title: viscum laxum var. abietis
intro: Viscum laxum var. abietis, also known as European mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of various tree species, such as fir and pine. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, European mistletoe is believed to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant is thought to have various health benefits, including reducing cancer cell growth, alleviating symptoms of chemotherapy, and supporting the overall well-being of individuals undergoing treatment. European mistletoe can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried extract, and is often used in traditional European folk medicine, particularly in Germany, where it is one of the most commonly prescribed medicinal plants.
